articles.title: Salmonella in apples and kiwi? Major UK supermarkets recall fruit products over potential contamination
articles.description: Major UK supermarkets including Tesco, Asda, and Sainsbury's are recalling apple and kiwi fruit packs due to potential Salmonella contamination. Shoppers are urged to check their fridges and return any affected products for a full refund. The recall was initiated by supplier Prepworld after detecting the bacteria. Food safety officials warn against consuming the fruit and advise seeking medical attention if symptoms arise.

articles.title: Clarke believes Scotland heading home after self-inflicted defeat
articles.description: MIAMI, June 24 : Scotland coach Steve Clarke thinks his team are heading home from their first World Cup in 28 years after what he viewed as a largely self-inflicted 3-0 loss to Brazil in their final Group C match on Wednesday. The Scots, with three points, still technically have a chance of progressing to t

articles.description: Rescuers race against time in Kolkata's Taratala warehouse collapse as five deaths are confirmed, an SIT is formed, and several workers are still feared trapped.

articles.title: Allentown Fire: Massive Blaze Triggers Fifth Alarm After Partial Building Collapse
articles.description: A massive fire ripped through a block-long three-storey structure in Allentown, Pennsylvania, prompting authorities to escalate the response to a fifth alarm.

articles.title: Narwekar apologises after criticism over tribute speech
articles.description: MNS chief Raj Thackeray had criticised Narwekar for what he termed the “butchering” of the Marathi language and for being unable to correctly read a prepared text.

articles.title: A dividend India must not squander
articles.description: The road to Viksit Bharat runs through India’s labour market. The country’s demographic dividend (DD) offers a rare opportunity to accelerate growth and prosperity. As the working-age share of the population rises, and the share of dependents (those below 15, and over 60 or 64 years) falls, several benefits emerge for the economy.

articles.description: Qualcomm’s investor day highlighted a major shift toward AI, with a new Meta partnership and strong long-term revenue targets across its core businesses.

articles.description: Owners must leash pets in public, use muzzles where required and carry poop bags. Registration costs ₹500, while existing licences remain valid.

articles.title: Strong Earthquake Strikes West Of Caracas, Venezuela
articles.description: A powerful 7.1-magnitude earthquake struck western Venezuela near Morón, with tremors felt across Caracas, the Caribbean, including Aruba and Curaçao.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Bosnia-Herzegovina boosts chances of advancing with 3-1 win over Qatar
articles.description: Bosnia-Herzegovina's 3-1 victory over Qatar enhances their World Cup advancement chances, marking a historic moment for the team.

articles.title: Global Market: China's 'future industries' investment boom sparks valuation bubble concerns
articles.description: China is witnessing a massive surge in venture capital, pouring billions into futuristic sectors like space, quantum computing, and AI. This investment frenzy, driven by Beijing's ambition to lead in technology and reduce reliance on foreign tech, sees even revenue-less startups attracting significant funding. Investor confidence is high, with new listing rules easing domestic market access for these promising, government-backed ventures.

articles.title: Best proverb of the day: 'When a rich man becomes poor he becomes a...' - this Chinese saying reveals how setbacks become powerful life lessons for others and why loss and failure can become your greatest teacher?
articles.description: Best proverb of the day: Explore the profound wisdom of a Chinese proverb that teaches how setbacks in wealth can lead to incredible life lessons, resilience, and teaching opportunities for those who have experienced both riches and poorness.

articles.title: Inspirational proverb of the day: “He who deliberates fully before taking a step will... — Life lessons on action, courage, overthinking and why a journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step
articles.description: Inspirational proverb of the day highlights the meaning of “He who deliberates fully before taking a step will spend his entire life on one leg.” The Chinese saying reflects how overthinking, hesitation, and excessive caution can sometimes prevent people from moving forward in life. It suggests that constant analysis of every possible outcome may lead to delay, causing missed opportunities for growth and progress.

articles.title: SFI names four-member Indian surfing team for 2026 Asian Games
articles.description: The Surfing Federation of India named a four-member team for surfing's debut at the 2026 Asian Games. Kishore Kumar, Sivaraj Babu, Kamali Moorthy, and Sugar Shanthi Banarse were selected based on performance in national and international events.

articles.title: Tata-STT's Delhi data centre suffers 'extensive damage'; data recovery challenged
articles.description: A significant fire at a New Delhi data centre, a joint venture between ST Telemedia and Tata Communications, has caused extensive damage, complicating data recovery efforts. One affected client, Matrix Cellular, reports losing two decades of crucial data. The incident also appears linked to recent intermittent Google Cloud network issues in India, leaving server rooms in ruins.

articles.title: Uttar Pradesh roadshow in Bengaluru nets Rs 50,000 crore in investment pledges
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath held a series of investor meetings in Bengaluru on Wednesday as part of a roadshow to attract investment into the state, which he says is now among India's top three investment destinations.

articles.title: IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant-Kuldeep Yadav trade 'good for both teams'
articles.description: India pacer Avesh Khan backs the Rishabh Pant-Kuldeep Yadav trade between Lucknow Super Giants and Delhi Capitals, calling it a beneficial move for both franchises. He believes Kuldeep's spin will be a significant asset for LSG at their home ground.

articles.description: The IDFC First Bank scam was of such magnitude that the CBI could not overlook it,” Mr. Hooda claimed.

articles.title: 40-year-old share trader found dead in city hotel, family alleges murder
articles.description: Jaipur trader Guru Prasad Chaudhary found dead in Narayan Vihar hotel room; police probe friends, Facebook Live video, and alcohol angle after family files FIR.

articles.title: SPG Director Alok Sharma gets nine-month service extension till 2027
articles.description: The Central government has extended the service of Special Protection Group (SPG) Director Alok Sharma by nine months. The 1991-batch IPS officer will now serve until March 31, 2027. The ACC approved the extension beyond his scheduled retirement.

articles.title: Govt pattas undercut crackdown on illegal colonies
articles.description: Rajasthan govt issues pattas via Urban Service Camps to regularise unauthorised colonies, easing inspections—officials warn it encourages encroachments and unsafe layouts.

articles.title: Mohan Yadav at centre of Ujjain land ‘scam’; BJP bombshell on Kharge family's trust: What is happening?
articles.description: A report showed that Mohan Yadav’s family bought 168 acres for ₹45 crore since December 2023, when the BJP leader become Madhya Pradesh chief minister.

articles.title: 'Everybody’s sick of you': New book claims Trump exploded at Netanyahu during heated Gaza ceasefire call
articles.description: A new book claims Trump exploded at Netanyahu during Gaza ceasefire talks in 2025. The reported outburst exposed deep private tensions despite their public alliance.

articles.title: Mysuru RPF officers trained on Jan Vishwas Act, 2026
articles.description: A briefing-cum-awareness session on the provisions of the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2026 and the consequential amendments to the Railways Act, 1989 and the Calcutta Metro Railway (Operation and Maintenance) Temporary Provisions Act, 1985 was conducted on Wednesday in Mysuru for officers and staff of the Railway Protection Force (RPF) of Mysuru and Hubballi divisions.

articles.title: China to enforce new ethnic unity law globally, says it has right to target 'separatists'
articles.description: The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law, which comes into force on July 1, seeks to strengthen what Beijing describes as a shared national identity among the country's ethnic groups. One provision extends potential legal liability to people and organisations outside China, an aspect that has drawn particular scrutiny. Rights advocates warn that the measure could provide fresh legal grounds for targeting overseas campaigners from communities such as the Uyghurs and Tibetans.

articles.title: `When Villages Progress, Punjab Progresses`: CM Bhagwant Singh Mann
articles.description: The AAP Chief said the decision reflects the Bhagwant Mann Government’s commitment to empowering grassroots democracy, strengthening rural governance and accelerating village development, as the government continues to take people-centric decisions aimed at building a prosperous and Rangla Punjab.

articles.title: Tripura suspends 8 policemen after HC observation in custodial torture & bribery case
articles.description: Tripura suspends 8 policemen after HC observation in custodial torture & bribery case

articles.title: 19 BJP councillors retake oath in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation after Kerala HC invalidates earlier ceremony
articles.description: 19 BJP councillors in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation have retaken their oaths following a High Court ruling that invalidated their previous ones. The court mandated strict adherence to the prescribed format, disallowing any additions like deities or "Mother India." This decision stems from challenges to oaths taken by 20 BJP councillors who had invoked Hindu deities and political martyrs. The BJP currently holds a majority in the 101-member civic body.

articles.title: School Education Minister reviews progress of various govt. schemes in Perambalur district
articles.description: School Education Minister Rajmohan reviews government schemes in Perambalur, emphasizing impact assessment and readiness for challenges like drought.

articles.title: ‘EVERYBODY HATES YOU!’: Trump Explodes At Netanyahu With ‘DIVORCE’ Threat In Secret Call | Report
articles.description: Did Donald Trump threaten to 'divorce' Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu during an angry phone call? A new book is shedding light on an alleged private clash between the two leaders, claiming what could be one of the most serious rifts in their long-standing political relationship. According to Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump by New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, Trump erupted during a September 2025 phone call over Netanyahu’s reluctance to accept a U.S.-brokered Gaza ceasefire plan. The book claims Trump told Netanyahu that “everybody hates you” and warned that continued opposition to the deal could lead to a “divorce” between the United States and Israel. The conversation reportedly took place in the presence of Trump envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner.

articles.title: UP emerged as revenue surplus state overcoming 'Bimaru' status, says CM Adityanath
articles.description: "For the last six years, Uttar Pradesh has been a revenue-surplus state. Rising from the bottom three, Uttar Pradesh is now counted among India's top three economies," CM said.

articles.title: How to Watch the Switzerland vs. Canada FIFA World Cup Game for Free
articles.description: Canada is within touching distance of their first-ever World Cup knockout game. Can they get the job done, or will Switzerland thwart their plans? Here's how to watch the game live using a streaming service or VPN.

articles.title: Vijay Deverakonda's Ranabaali director on casting The Mummy actor Arnold Vosloo: 'I was star-struck'
articles.description: Director Rahul Sankrityan has opened up about casting The Mummy star Arnold Vosloo as the antagonist in Vijay Deverakonda's upcoming historical epic Ranabaali. The filmmaker recalled being star-struck upon meeting the Hollywood actor.

articles.title: Elevated roads and tunnel to link Vasant Kunj, Dwarka and Gurgaon
articles.description: A significant Rs 7,000-crore tunnel project, along with approach roads, is poised for Union Cabinet approval. This ambitious infrastructure will connect Nelson Mandela Marg to the Shiv Murti interchange, promising seamless travel to Terminal 3, Gurgaon, and Dwarka Expressways. The six-lane tunnel aims to bypass the congested Mahipalpur stretch, significantly reducing travel time and improving traffic flow for commuters across various parts of Delhi and beyond.

articles.title: India's FY27 growth likely to slow down to 6.6% on energy stress, sub-par monsoon: S&P
articles.description: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in the current fiscal year, according to S&P Global Ratings. This slowdown is attributed to energy market pressures, a potentially weak monsoon, and a global economic deceleration. Higher energy and fertilizer costs are expected to fuel inflation, potentially leading to a policy rate hike in the latter half of the fiscal year.

articles.title: Kerala govt plans multi-layer defence against human-wildlife conflict: Forest Minister
articles.description: Kerala plans a multi-layer defense against human-wildlife conflict using AI surveillance, early warnings, and innovative deterrent measures.

articles.description: AP EAPCET Result 2026 expected soon; rank card link to be activated online.

articles.title: Aries, Taurus, Gemini Horoscope Today for June 25, 2026: Partnership Luck Rises, Workplace Breakthroughs and Creative Wins Await
articles.description: Your Daily Horoscope Today emphasizes team collaboration, workspace organization, and vibrant creative expression. It is a powerful day to prioritize diplomatic negotiations, disciplined budgeting, and research-backed decisions over impulsive actions to secure long-term personal and financial stability. Chandresh Sharma predicts in today’s horoscope and how it can shape your emotional and professional outlook.

articles.title: Calcutta High Court refuses urgent hearing in cases relating to Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee
articles.description: Calcutta High Court denies urgent hearing for Abhishek Banerjee's medical travel, citing ongoing cases and normal legal procedures.
